question.label: Landing check-in
question.name: What's felt most clear or unclear for you in week two, Riley?
question.description: Lets Riley set the current onboarding picture before the conversation gets specific.

answer: I am comfortable reading the services, but I am less sure how decisions are really made here.
note: He has technical reading confidence but lacks a clear picture of how decisions get made, which is a mild clarity gap.

question.label: Decision-making map
question.name: When you say 'how decisions are really made' — what part is least visible?
question.description: Drills into the specific decision-making gap he named so the manager can remove that onboarding ambiguity.

answer: I can trace most request paths now, though I still miss historical context behind certain tradeoffs.
note: He has a workable technical map but still lacks the historical context behind tradeoffs, which is a mild remaining clarity gap.

question.label: Historical tradeoffs
question.name: Which tradeoffs feel hardest to place without the backstory?
question.description: Drills into the specific missing historical context he named so the manager can target the gap.

answer: I have not been fully clear on who is on point for each domain when priorities conflict.
note: He names a specific clarity gap: unclear domain ownership when priorities conflict.

question.label: Domain ownership
question.name: When priorities conflict, where does domain ownership get murky for you?
question.description: Drills into the specific ownership-conflict gap he named so the manager can map the unclear domains.

answer: I am trying not to interrupt people, so sometimes I keep questions in a notes doc instead of asking.
note: He is self-censoring questions to avoid interrupting people, which strongly signals unclear asking norms and blocked access to context. [THREAD-DEFERRED] 'keep questions in a notes doc instead of asking' could be worth revisiting next session.

question.label: Context moments
question.name: When do you find yourself needing more context before you move?
question.description: Identifies the decision points where he should slow down and ask instead of filling gaps himself.

answer: I would benefit from a clearer guide for what to escalate quickly versus what to batch.
note: Names a specific missing guide for escalation versus batching, which is a strong current blocker and signals hesitation to move without clearer norms.

question.label: Escalation guide
question.name: What would a useful escalate-versus-batch guide need to cover?
question.description: Turns the missing escalation guide into concrete criteria the manager can help provide.

answer: The runbook helped, but some practical handoff details only show up during live incidents.
note: He names a specific missing context source — practical handoff details only visible in live incidents — which is a strong clarity gap and mild drag on engagement.

question.label: Live incidents
question.name: Which handoff details only become visible during live incidents?
question.description: Drills into the specific incident handoff gaps so the manager can make tacit operating norms explicit.

answer: A short weekly architecture review with someone senior would help me calibrate faster.
note: Names a specific missing support structure — regular senior architecture review — indicating a strong current clarity gap and friction that is making onboarding less sustainable. [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] Weekly architecture review with someone senior.

question.label: Useful introductions
question.name: Who would be most useful for you to spend live time with next?
question.description: Helps line up the next relationship that will improve his map of how the team actually works.

answer: If I know exactly what ownership I should take this month, I can contribute with more confidence.
note: He pivoted from introductions to needing clear ownership this month, signaling mild uncertainty that is dampening confidence; [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] ownership this month.

question.label: Steady next steps
question.name: What should we line up before our next check-in?
question.description: Closes the meeting by turning the discussion into concrete follow-up and support.

answer: I will ask one architecture question in the next incident review instead of keeping it in my notes doc.
note: He named a specific next step to ask an architecture question in a live forum, showing clearer intent and active learning behavior.
